calendar.matters:
Petition For Recovery Of Funds On Deposit In County Treasury.
Off Calendar Oct-22-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Robert Prohaska.

The Court inquired whether there was a birth certificate for Frank W. Mr. Boessenecker addressed Frank Charles Prohaska. The Court stated that there were three marriages, that Frank W. was born to Frank C. and Lucille, and that it missed the birth certificate of Frank W. showing him as the Son of Frank C. and Lucille. In addition, the Court stated that there were other issues such as Frank J. being Robert's Father who was born in Nebraska and the issue with the birthplace of Frank C. The Court took a brief recess on the matter for Mr. Boessenecker to review his exhibits. Back on the record at the second call of the matter, Mr. Boessenecker referred the Court to the document located at exhibit three at about the fifteenth page, stated that he did not find a birth certificate for Frank William Prohaska but he did find the SS5 which is supplied by the geneologist, that the SS5 is submitted in lieu of a birth certificate, and that it is usually accepted by the Courts. In addition, Mr. Boessenecker addressed the spelling discrepancies of Prohaska and addressed the issue with the census showing the birthplace of Frank C. as Bohemia, the Czech Republic, and Austria/Austro-Hungary. The Court inquired about the other possible children that might take regarding the escheated funds and noted that according to the Government Code that it appears to be different. Mr. Boessenecker stated that it is different, that as to escheated funds that it is first come first served, and that the petitioner is entitled to the whole. Mr. Boessenecker referred the Court to his 6/29/10 petition at paragraph three. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.
